description_long: This object is a vintage-style television with a boxy, rectangular body and a prominent, slightly recessed black screen on the front. The casing is primarily dark gray, with a brownish-orange section on the top that supports two thin, angled antenna rods. On the right side of the front panel, there are two large, round control knobs stacked vertically, and a small rectangular gold panel beneath them, likely for additional controls or a speaker grille. The back of the television is solid and tapers inward, typical of older cathode ray tube (CRT) designs, and the sides are plain with no visible ports or features. The overall design is utilitarian, with sharp edges and a sturdy, retro appearance. This television would be commonly found in urban settings such as old apartments, garages, or curbside for disposal.
description: A retro, box-shaped television with a dark gray body, black screen, brown antenna mount, and two prominent control knobs on the front right.
description_view_0: Front view showing the black screen, two round control knobs, a gold panel, and the brown antenna base with antennas extending upward.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain, solid gray side and the angled back, with the antenna base visible on top.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the tapered, solid back and the brown antenna base with antennas, with no visible ports or features.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the plain gray side and the angled back, similar to the left side, with the antenna base on top.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, dark gray plastic bezel with a matte finish and sharp edges
attribute_control_button_layout: two large round knobs and a small rectangular button cluster on the front right panel
